title: Bidens carinata
taxonomy: Bidens carinata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Bidens and is also known as the rough biddens or rough beggar's lice. The species Bidens carinata is native to tropical Asia and is widely distributed in various regions. It is often found growing in damp or wet environments, such as marshes and along riverbanks. Bidens carinata is an annual or perennial herb that can grow up to several feet tall.
uses: Bidens carinata, also known as East African tickseed, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is also used to make a tea that is believed to have antiseptic and antioxidant properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Bidens carinata.
benefits: Bidens carinata, also known as the spiny beggartick or spiny tickseed, has been found to possess various health benefits. Its leaves and roots have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have potential in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
